name: Long Island Housing Partnership, INC.
description_short: Non-profit housing counselor providing free default prevention, affordable rental programs, homeownership assistance, and rehabilitation services across Long Island.
description_long:
Long Island Housing Partnership, Inc. is a non-profit organization established to address affordable housing shortages on Long Island by providing accessible housing opportunities to individuals and families who cannot secure decent housing through standard market channels. The organization operates from their Hauppage headquarters and maintains business hours of 9am–5pm, Monday through Friday, with language services available upon request.

LIHP offers a comprehensive suite of housing-related services organized into five core categories: Rentals (managing multiple affordable apartment complexes across Long Island), Homeownership (programs like the Employer Assisted Housing Program), Rehabilitation (home improvement assistance), Arrears (default and foreclosure prevention counseling), and Counseling & Education. Their active programs include over a dozen specific rental developments such as Village by the Bay (Amityville), Village Square (Glen Cove), Fairfield Greens South (Holbrook), and Bayport Gardens (senior homeownership). They also administer the Town of Riverhead Accessory Dwelling Unit Program to expand affordable housing stock.

What distinguishes LIHP is their dual focus on both rental affordability and homeownership pathways. Rather than offering a single service, they function as a comprehensive housing development and counseling organization that directly manages or partners with property owners on multiple residential communities. Their Default and Foreclosure Prevention Counseling is explicitly free, government-funded, and they publicly warn consumers against predatory scam companies that charge upfront fees—a significant consumer protection stance.

LIHP is best suited for Long Island residents seeking affordable rental housing, first-time homebuyers in high-cost areas, individuals facing mortgage default or foreclosure, and employers seeking to help recruit and retain employees. The organization's track record involves managing substantial real estate portfolios and government partnerships, though specific outcomes, wait lists, or income qualification details are not disclosed on their website.
pros: Completely free default and foreclosure prevention counseling funded by government and philanthropic sources
Directly manages or partners on 12+ active affordable housing developments across diverse Long Island communities
Offers employer-assisted homeownership programs that match employer contributions with public funding
Provides language services upon request to serve non-English speaking populations
Accessible business hours (9am–5pm weekdays) with multiple contact methods (phone, email, in-person)
Explicitly warns consumers against predatory scam companies charging upfront fees for foreclosure help
Programs span entire housing lifecycle: rentals, homeownership, rehabilitation, and financial counseling
cons: Website lacks specific income qualification thresholds, making it unclear who is eligible
No published wait list information or application timelines for any rental programs
Limited transparency on success rates, number of counseling clients served, or program outcomes
Employer Assisted Housing Program appears limited to businesses with existing partnerships
No information about whether counseling is available via phone/online or requires in-person visits
best_for: Long Island homeowners facing mortgage default, foreclosure, or payment difficulties
Low-to-moderate income renters seeking affordable apartments in Nassau and Suffolk counties
First-time homebuyers and employees of participating employers in high-cost Long Island market
Seniors seeking affordable senior-specific housing communities (Bayport Gardens, Fairfield Knolls)
diagnosis: Long Island Housing Partnership is a legitimate non-profit best for Long Island residents seeking affordable housing or facing foreclosure—not a credit repair or lending service. The organization's primary caveat is lack of transparency about eligibility criteria, wait times, and program outcomes, making preliminary phone contact (631-435-4710) essential before assuming availability.
services: Default and Foreclosure Prevention Counseling (free, government-funded)
Affordable rental housing application processing for 12+ residential complexes
Homeownership program administration and financing assistance
Employer Assisted Housing Program (employer matching contributions for home purchase)
Home rehabilitation assistance and program management
Rent arrears counseling and prevention services
Accessory Dwelling Unit (ADU) program administration (Town of Riverhead)
First-come, first-served application processing for affordable units
Senior affordable housing programs and condominiums
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
meta_description: Non-profit housing counselor providing free default prevention, affordable rental programs, and homeownership assistance.
